title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers argparse interface, help, options, and usage errors into a changed testable user-facing command interface. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of testable user-facing command interface with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.
Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Argument Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 5564 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to define and validate a reproducible task-oriented argparse interface with useful help; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Argument Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Argument Model, keep Argument Model connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-python314-argparse-tia0vt
# Competency: bookbot-python314-argparse.
# Case 8637: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_python314_argparse_tia0vt(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_tia0vt = "tia0vt-tv0ait-8637"
    assert evidence_variant_tia0vt
    evidence_axes_tia0vt = (
        "corpustia0vt",
        "policytv0ait",
        "revision8637",
        "commandtia0vt8637",
        "reporttv0ait8637",
        "test8637tia0vt",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_tia0vt)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"
